text.title: DataLoader: N+1 Prevention
text.summary: DataLoader batches and deduplicates DB calls within a request to eliminate the N+1 query problem.

rule.statement: Instantiate DataLoaders per-request in the context object. Never create a global DataLoader shared across requests.
rule.rationale: A global DataLoader accumulates keys from all concurrent requests, producing incorrect results by mixing data across users. Per-request loaders are isolated and garbage-collected after the response.

rule.statement: Pair every resolver that loads a related entity by parent ID with a DataLoader. The batch function must return results in the same order as the input keys.
rule.rationale: If the batch function returns results in DB order rather than input-key order, DataLoader maps results to wrong parents, producing silently incorrect data.

section.title: TypeScript DataLoader Factory
section.reference: TypeScript DataLoader factory with grouping pattern

section.title: GraphQL Federation
section.p: For federated microservices, use Apollo Federation's `@key` directive. Each service resolves its own entities; the gateway stitches them without N+1.
